title: plantago coronopus var. commutata
intro: Plantago coronopus var. commutata, also known as narrow-leaved plantain, is a plant species within the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) characterized by its narrow leaves and small white flowers. Traditionally, narrow-leaved plantain has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of certain flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The medicinal properties of this plant are believed to provide various health benefits, including wound healing, antiseptic properties, and relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Narrow-leaved plantain can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
